title: High-dimensional regression
summary: Oracle inequalities for the LASSO estimator in sparse high-dimensional linear models, bounding prediction and estimation error under the Restricted Eigenvalue condition.

theorems.name: LASSO solution (minimizer of the LASSO objective)
theorems.blurb: Defines the LASSO estimator as a minimizer of the squared-error plus L1-penalty objective over all coefficient vectors.

theorems.name: LASSO oracle prediction-error bound
theorems.blurb: Under the Restricted Eigenvalue condition RE(s,3,κ) and a noise control on λ, the in-sample prediction error of the LASSO is bounded by a sparsity-scaled multiple of λ².

theorems.name: LASSO oracle L1 estimation bound
theorems.blurb: The L1 estimation error of the LASSO is bounded by a sparsity-scaled multiple of λ under the Restricted Eigenvalue condition.

theorems.name: LASSO oracle L2 (support-restricted) bound
theorems.blurb: The squared L2 estimation error restricted to the true support S is bounded by a sparsity-scaled function of λ and the RE constant κ.

theorems.name: LASSO cone condition
theorems.blurb: Any LASSO solution's error vector lies in the cone where off-support L1 mass is at most three times the on-support L1 mass, enabling the RE condition to apply.
